notes: Stakeholder data for these evals lives in enterprise-architect/syntethic-data/ (markdown entries with `type: stakeholder` frontmatter). Each eval prompt is what a real architect would type; the agent is told the repo path as context, the way a wiki CLAUDE.md would expose it.

evals.name: cloud-data-migration
evals.prompt: I'm scoping a migration of our analytics data platform off on-prem into the cloud (AWS to Azure for the data warehouse). Some of it is customer financial data. Who should I be talking to before we commit to this?
evals.expected_output: Shortlist that includes Anika Sharma (data architecture), Carlos Mendes (cloud), and Charlotte Dubois (compliance — regulated/customer data), each with a rationale and a 1-10 score, ordered by score. Payments-only people not top-scored without a structural reason.
assertions: Includes Anika Sharma (Head of Data Architecture)
Includes Carlos Mendes (Cloud Architect)
Includes Charlotte Dubois (Compliance) because customer financial data is in scope
Every listed stakeholder has a relevancy score from 1 to 10
Every listed stakeholder has a written rationale naming the relevance axis
Stakeholders are ordered by relevancy score, highest first
No fabricated people outside the six synthetic-data entries
Payments specialists not top-scored (8-10) without an explicit structural reason

evals.name: instant-payments-modernisation
evals.prompt: We need to modernise our payment rails to support SEPA instant payments and the PSD2 deadlines are coming. Who are the stakeholders I need to align with?
evals.expected_output: Shortlist led by Bram Hendriks and Aisha Khan (payments), with Charlotte Dubois (compliance) and possibly Ahmed Benali, each with rationale and 1-10 score, ordered by score.
assertions: Includes Bram Hendriks (Domain Architect — Payments)
Includes Aisha Khan (Tribe Lead — Payments)
Includes Charlotte Dubois (Compliance) for PSD2
Bram Hendriks and/or Aisha Khan scored highest (8-10)
Every listed stakeholder has a relevancy score from 1 to 10
Every listed stakeholder has a written rationale naming the relevance axis
Stakeholders are ordered by relevancy score, highest first
No fabricated people outside the six synthetic-data entries

evals.name: api-gateway-event-backbone
evals.prompt: Who do I need to talk to about standardising integration across domains — we're proposing a managed API gateway and a shared event backbone, and I want to retire a load of point-to-point batch interfaces.
evals.expected_output: Ahmed Benali (integration) as the clear top-scored lead; possibly Carlos and domain architects with stated reasons. Scores and rationale per person; only relevant people.
assertions: Includes Ahmed Benali (Integration Architect)
Ahmed Benali is the highest-scored stakeholder (8-10)
Every listed stakeholder has a relevancy score from 1 to 10
Every listed stakeholder has a written rationale naming the relevance axis
Stakeholders are ordered by relevancy score, highest first
No fabricated people outside the six synthetic-data entries
Only genuinely relevant people listed; off-topic people omitted or low-scored

evals.name: design-payments-working-session
evals.prompt: We're running a hands-on technical design session to work out the instant-payments processing flow and how it integrates with our systems. Who should be in the room?
evals.expected_output: Design/working altitude: makers up (Ahmed, Bram, Aisha as delivery voice); 'Head of' roles (Carlos, Anika) pushed down as too senior; unprofiled hands-on builders flagged as missing. Mode/altitude stated.
assertions: States the meeting type / altitude it sized the room for (design / working / maker level)
Surfaces Ahmed Benali and Bram Hendriks as hands-on design participants
Aisha Khan included at a working-relevant score, not dropped for being junior
Does not elevate anyone to the top purely on seniority; a 'Head of' is only high if core
Flags that the actual hands-on builders (unprofiled reports) are missing from the wiki
Scores 1-10 with rationale, ordered; no fabricated people in the ranked list

evals.name: strategy-payments-investment-case (toggle: INCLUSIVE expected)
evals.prompt: We're holding a steering session to set the multi-year strategy and the investment case for modernising our payments platform. Who should be in the room?
evals.expected_output: Strategic altitude, INCLUSIVE room-breadth mode (the 'investment case' framing keeps a delivery-feasibility voice). Bram top; Aisha kept below him as the feasibility voice; engineer level represented-by; unprofiled senior chain in gap note. Mode stated.
assertions: States the meeting type AND which room-breadth mode it used (tight vs inclusive)
Selects INCLUSIVE mode and justifies it from the investment-case framing
Surfaces Bram Hendriks as the senior payments-architecture voice
Keeps Aisha Khan as a delivery-feasibility voice ranked below Bram (not collapsed)
Collapses the engineer level to a 'represented by' note
Flags the unprofiled senior chain (Mark van Dijk / Jeroen Meijer / Miguel Santos) in the gap note
Scores 1-10 with rationale, ordered; no fabricated people in the ranked list

evals.name: strategy-payments-direction-only (toggle: TIGHT expected)
evals.prompt: We're convening a leadership steering session to set the multi-year direction and priorities for payments — purely the strategic direction and mandate, not a delivery or costing exercise. Who should be in the room?
evals.expected_output: Strategic altitude, TIGHT (leadership-only) room-breadth mode (no deliverability signal). Bram is the single payments-line voice; Aisha collapsed to represented-by; unprofiled senior chain in gap note as the likely chair/sign-off. Mode stated.
assertions: States the meeting type AND which room-breadth mode it used (tight vs inclusive)
Selects TIGHT mode and justifies it from the direction-only framing
Surfaces Bram Hendriks as the single payments-line voice in the ranked list
Aisha Khan collapsed to a 'represented by Bram' note, not ranked at top altitude
Ranked list has one voice per payments stream (no domain-architect + tribe-lead stacking)
Flags the unprofiled senior chain in the gap note as the likely chair/sign-off
Scores 1-10 with rationale, ordered; no fabricated people in the ranked list

evals.name: data-product-design-deep-chain (one voice per stream, full depth)
evals.prompt: We're running a hands-on design session for a new regulatory risk-reporting data product — we need to nail the data model, how it's built, and how it's governed. Who should be in the room?
evals.expected_output: Design altitude. The deep data-architecture chain (Fatima -> Anika -> Lotte -> Eva, one concern) collapses to ONE voice (Eva, the maker); Lotte/Anika/Fatima represented-by. Separate seats only for genuine remit forks: data-engineering (Sofia), governance (Floor), compliance (Charlotte). Table carries a Stream column; no two rows share a stream tag. Regression test for the levels-not-streams failure.
assertions: Sizes it as a design / working / maker-level session and states so
The data-architecture stream contributes exactly ONE voice (Eva Timmermans); Lotte Peters is NOT also ranked
Lotte Peters, Anika Sharma and Fatima El-Youssef are 'represented by' (not in the ranked list)
A separate data-engineering voice (Sofia Costa or David Cohen) appears as a genuine remit fork
A separate data-governance voice (Floor van der Berg) appears as a genuine remit fork
No two ranked rows share the same stream tag (Stream column present)
Table with full name, role, stream, rationale, 1-10 score; no fabricated people in the ranked list
